methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $14M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Transportation. Range: $270M - $1.8B.

data.narrative:
The ePlane Company is a Series B transportation-tech firm founded by Satya Chakravarthy, currently raising $40–50M on $14M+ in prior capital. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 3 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies (Einride at $1.8B Series C, Harbinger at $500M Series C, REGENT Craft at $270M Series A), with no public comparables available in the narrower frontier aerospace-transportation segment.

Two valuation methods anchor the analysis. Stage-progression applies a typical 1.9x step-up multiplier to the $14M total-raised proxy, implying $27M. Peer-median leverages the three identified comps; their median valuation is $500M, with a range spanning $270M to $1.8B. The stage dispersion (one Series A, two Series C) introduces material variance but reflects the limited depth of available comparables in this subsector.

Triangulating these methods yields a low estimate of $23M, a median of $263M, and a high of $575M. The wide spread reflects fundamental uncertainty: the stage-progression method produces a materially lower result anchored to raised capital, while peer-median reflects the substantial valuations commanded by adjacent transportation-tech platforms at later stages. Key uncertainties center on the appropriate stage-adjustment factor, the relevance of Series C comps to a Series B round, and early-stage execution risk in a capital-intensive frontier sector. A $150M–$350M range may be most defensible pending clarity on unit economics and path to profitability.
